mainEntity.name: What is answer engine optimization (AEO) and how is it different from regular SEO?

acceptedAnswer.text:
Answer engine optimization (AEO) is the practice of structuring your content so AI systems like ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, and AI Overviews can understand it and quote it directly in their answers. It’s less about ranking pages and more about being the trusted source answer engines pull from.

AEO starts with researching the actual questions your audience asks, then writing concise, answer-first content and marking it up with appropriate schema so AI crawlers can parse and reuse it. Compared with traditional SEO, it focuses on conversational queries, Q&A formats, and machine readability.

Key factors:
- Question-first content strategy and concise Q&A blocks
- Use of schema.org types like FAQPage, HowTo, Article, Organization
- Technical crawlability for AI bots (GPTBot, PerplexityBot, etc.)
- Strong authority and trust signals (reviews, expertise, consistency)

Begin by listing 20–50 core questions your buyers ask, converting them into clear headings, leading with 40–60 word answers, and adding FAQ/schema markup. Review regularly as AI platforms evolve.

Done well, AEO quietly turns your site into a reliable data source that answer engines prefer to cite.
acceptedAnswer.description: Answer engine optimization (AEO) is about making your content easily understandable and quotable by AI answer engines like ChatGPT and Perplexity. Instead of chasing rankings, AEO focuses on question-first content, Q&A formatting, and schema markup so answer engines can lift your answers directly and cite your brand.
